title: Design non-residue specification pattern for tower extensions
description:
Define how `QuadraticExt` and `CubicExt` know which irreducible polynomial to use at each tower level. ## Decision Use a **config trait** (arkworks-style). A zero-sized marker type per extension level specifies `BaseField` and `NON_RESIDUE`. This is zero-cost, handles nested towers naturally, and is the industry standard. Rejected: const-generic (dead end for nested towers where β is an extension field element) and runtime (per-element overhead, breaks `ConstField`). ## Deliverable `crates/gf2-core/src/gfpn/ext_config.rs` containing: - `ExtConfig` trait with `type BaseField: ConstField`, `const NON_RESIDUE: Self::BaseField`, and `fn mul_by_non_residue()` with default impl. - Unit tests verifying a concrete config compiles and `mul_by_non_residue` works for a simple `Fp

` base field. See design plan: `dev/plans/non_residue_config_trait.md` ## Success criteria - `ExtConfig` trait compiles and can be implemented for `Fp<7>` with β = -1. - `mul_by_non_residue` default impl matches manual multiplication. - A nested config (where `BaseField` is itself an extension type) compiles — verified once `QuadraticExt` exists (tracked in 8d2863e6), but the trait definition must not preclude it. - No runtime overhead: config types are zero-sized.
